title: FloodLAMP Inactivation Protocol v3.1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"caeuc","text":"The inactivation protocol uses a chemical plus heat to break open cells (including virus, if present) and preserve the RNA. The inactivation can be done outside a lab setting, at the point of sample collection or drop-off. This is our preferred mode, and we refer to these as \"inactivation stations\". Prior to inactivation, the samples may contain live, contagious virus, so it is crucial that personnel use proper PPE and safe handling procedures. Many current community screening efforts are performing non-lab based sample processing (for example, Dave O'Connor and Chris Mason), and we have followed their examples with the use of splash guards in addition to PPE.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":551,"length":13},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":569,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":551,"length":13},{"key":1,"offset":569,"length":11}],"data":[]},{"key":"10a1l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3n241","text":"After inactivation, samples should be refrigerated or stored on ice before same-day processing through the assay.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fgbff","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9cq4b","text":"On our website are our protocols in worksheet form as we use in the lab. This and more information will be live soon.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":7,"length":7}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B11EBE04F21311EA92F30A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://dho.pathology.wisc.edu"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B11EBE62F21311EA92F30A58A9FEAC2A","url":"http://masonlab.net"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B9D84E1BF21311EA92F30A58A9FEAC2A","url":"http://floodlamp.bio"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5jjl6","text":"Individuals are responsible for the chemical and bio safety training to safely complete this protocol.  Be careful of aerating samples pre-inactivation. The inactivation solution should also be handled cautiously. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f3gs3","text":"- Set Up: turn on dry heater, get out fozen  lab armor, turn on centrifuge, make temp QC tube for each size and volume of tube going through inactivation process","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ar6p","text":"- Safety Procedures: always cover heater with plastic cover, wear appropriate PPE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4r9sl","text":"Both TCEP and EDTA should be handled cautiously as they can cause severe eye damage and are toxic if inhaled. See SDS for TCEP, EDTA, NaOH, HCl and NaI for more safety information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: For dry nasal swabs add 2.5mL 1xPBS, soak swabs for 2minutes, remove and discard swabs properly
Spray all closed tubes with 70% ethanol
Vortex the 100x Inactivation Solution within five minutes of use
Add 100x Inactivation Solution to collection tube, add 50uL per 5mL collected sample
For positive controls, spike selected samples with inactivated virions
For each sample, vortex for 5 seconds, shake and then vortex for another 5 seconds
Heat the samples at 95C for 8 minutes, be sure to add the QC tube(s) for in situ temperature check
Chill samples using the frozen lab armor for 4 minutes, alteratively on ice
Check caps are tight, then Centrifuge samples at 5K rpm for 4 minutes
Aspirate the clarified samples into new tubes, be sure not to disturb any solids at the bottom of the original tube
creator: Randy True
